purpose: Reproducible fresh-agent acceptance cases for the GA4 tracking-plan skill.

cases.title: Whole-site ecommerce and customer-space plan
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Create a human-readable GA4 and GTM tracking plan for https://shop.example.com with no client template. Scope is the whole website. Supplied browser evidence confirms home, navigation, search, product lists with color and size filters, product detail with variant switching and availability, cart, checkout, payment refusal, purchase confirmation, signup, login, order history, order detail, return start, confirmed order cancellation, profile update, preferences, wishlist, and reorder. Screenshots are required, but Playwright MCP is unavailable in this evaluation environment. Decide all other details as a web analyst.

required_outcomes.description: Events and parameters form coherent public, commerce, checkout, and authenticated journeys tied to business and analysis needs.
Website, workbook, and controlled-value languages are explicitly decided; technical event and parameter names remain English.
The supplied browser evidence and unavailable Playwright attempt are recorded separately for journey and finite-value discovery.

GA4 ecommerce meanings, canonical trigger wording, types, event-specific requiredness, examples, and parameter conditions are resolved from pinpointed official Google sections checked on the publication date.

The complete official table is reviewed internally for each selected event. Every mandatory and applicable conditional field is retained; plausible unresolved conditional fields remain visible with a confirmation or development dependency. Other applicable official fields are preferentially included when website evidence, recurrent analysis, activation, or a feasible source supports them, without mechanically copying every field. Category levels four and five require evidenced taxonomy depth and use.

Every custom event, item, or user parameter identifies the official fields reviewed, the specific official gap, business question, reporting purpose, scope, source, registration decision, cardinality, privacy, availability, and owner. Item size is custom and official item_variant is considered first.

Purchase retains stable, useful list, shipping, payment, or other checkout context only when it can be persisted reliably to the order. Each propagated binding records event-specific classification, source, retention/fallback/reset rule, and owner; payment_type and shipping_tier are not presented as prescribed purchase fields.

Each finite filter, variant, payment type, and shipping tier value carries supplied observed or client-confirmed evidence and any translation mapping; dynamic product IDs and names use rules rather than exhaustive lists.
Event summaries, triggers, and parameter definitions are concise and concrete; generic reusable, applicable, or official-GA4 filler is absent.
Payment refusal, confirmed cancellation, and refund are modeled as distinct outcomes with appropriate event judgement.
Variant availability is considered on view_item and is not added indiscriminately to list and selection events.
Authenticated events rely only on the supplied observed customer-space evidence.
Required screenshots are visibly blocked with the unavailable Playwright MCP attempt and an analyst-facing notice.
The main delivery is a readable XLSX with five core human tabs plus Screenshot Register because screenshots are required, without agent-only fields in visible tabs.

prohibited_outcomes.description: Universal Analytics fields or event model are proposed.
purchase is used for a failed payment.
The workbook claims complete screenshot evidence or silently omits the blocked state.

cases.title: Distinct lead outcomes
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Design the GA4 event model for https://services.example.com. Observed successful outcomes are newsletter subscription, contact request, catalogue request, and appointment request. Marketing reports newsletter growth separately, sales owns contact and appointment leads, and operations owns catalogue fulfilment. No client naming convention exists. Screenshots are explicitly excluded.

required_outcomes.description: The plan explicitly decides whether generate_lead can consolidate outcomes and explains the business ownership tradeoff.
Separate governed success events are used where reporting meaning and ownership differ.
Reusable form and lead parameters are governed without free-text or personal-data leakage.
Screenshot requirement is not_requested, no screenshot evidence rows are fabricated, and Screenshot Register is omitted from the workbook.
Live browser exploration is still attempted for journey and finite-value discovery even though screenshots are excluded.

prohibited_outcomes.description: generate_lead and separate success events fire for the same outcome without explicit duplicate governance.
Captured screenshot files or a Playwright attempt are invented.

cases.title: Formula-bearing client workbook
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Adapt a GA4 plan to a client workbook. The inspection inventory reports formulas, sheet protection, Excel tables, data validations, comments, and images on the Event Matrix sheet. The client identifies writable cells inside the existing event blocks and provides the inspected template SHA-256 but has not approved structural extension. Every other cell and workbook feature must remain unchanged.

required_outcomes.description: The template inventory is treated as a precondition to adaptation.
The writable-cell mapping is bound to the exact inspected template SHA-256 and stops if the source workbook differs.
Strict adaptation writes only the explicitly approved cells and does not rebuild, replace, clone, create, or delete a sheet.
An artifact-bound fidelity report compares the saved output with the source template and delivery stops on any difference outside approved value or explicitly approved formula cells.
All other cells, sheets, formulas, styles, merges, dimensions, validations, tables, comments, images, protection, and print settings remain unchanged.

prohibited_outcomes.description: Formulas or protected workbook features are changed outside an explicitly approved formula cell.
A legacy mapping or whole-sheet compatibility path is presented as strict client-template adaptation.
Destructive approval is inferred from the request.

cases.title: Required screenshot capture is unavailable
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. A GA4 plan has already been designed for https://content.example.com and screenshots are required. Tool discovery confirms Playwright MCP is unavailable and the eligible fallback browser cannot capture the interaction. No screenshots were supplied.

required_outcomes.description: The actual Playwright MCP and fallback outcome is recorded rather than inferred from preflight alone.
Affected Screenshot Register rows use blocked with concrete reasons.
The workbook and final reply both show the blocked capture notice.
The measurement model remains explicit about observed versus inferred event evidence.

prohibited_outcomes.description: capture_required or skip_allowed is used as a final state.
A filename or captured status is invented.

cases.title: Reusable navigation and multilingual values
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Create a GA4 interaction model for a multilingual corporate website at https://business.example.com. The client prefers separate header, menu, submenu, and footer events. Observed French labels include Services, Equipe, A propos, Contact, and Mentions legales. The website will add English and German markets next year. Screenshots are explicitly excluded.

required_outcomes.description: Reusable surface events follow the client preference instead of forcing select_content for navigation.
The workbook and controlled-value languages are explicitly English because the plan must scale across French, English, and German markets.
Controlled values are English lowercase ASCII snake_case and reusable across markets.
Observed finite values are exhausted below the governed threshold, each value is linked to supplied evidence and translation mapping, and values render with readable pipe separators.
Navigation parameters support future markets without embedding translated labels as event names.

prohibited_outcomes.description: Accented or space-separated translated labels are used as controlled analytics values.
A separate event name is created for every individual navigation link.

cases.title: French-only human tracking plan
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Create a focused GA4 and GTM tracking plan for a French-only retail website at https://fr.example.com. The client team, website, and supplied workbook template use French only. Observed finite navigation values are Nouveautes, Pret a porter, Chaussures, and Espace client. Keep official GA4 naming and all technical event and parameter identifiers in English. Screenshots are explicitly excluded.

required_outcomes.description: The workbook uses French human labels, summaries, triggers, and parameter explanations.
GA4 and custom event names, parameter names, dataLayer keys, and wrappers remain English lowercase technical identifiers.
French semantic values use lowercase ASCII snake_case without accents, for example nouveautes and pret_a_porter.
Localized official wording retains the catalog-validated canonical Google definition and pinpointed official source zones.
Each finite navigation value is marked observed or client-confirmed with its own source reference, not inferred from a parameter-level note.

prohibited_outcomes.description: Official or custom technical event and parameter names are translated into French.
Controlled analytics values contain accents, spaces, or title case.
French wording replaces the canonical official Google meaning without retaining source-grounded truth.

cases.title: Blocked customer-space discovery
cases.prompt: Start with no prior context. Create a whole-site GA4 and GTM tracking plan for https://retail.example.com. Public evidence confirms an ecommerce storefront, account registration, login, product discovery, cart, and checkout. Playwright MCP attempts public registration with synthetic information, but a CAPTCHA blocks authentication before any customer-space page renders. No client evidence describes the gated capabilities. Screenshots are explicitly excluded. Produce a complete specification without presenting unobserved customer-space behavior as fact.

required_outcomes.description: The synthetic authentication attempt, CAPTCHA boundary, and absence of rendered gated evidence are recorded precisely.
Applicable recurrent customer-space outcomes are visibly recommended with low or medium confidence and site data marked to_confirm.
Recommended events use precise success or backend-confirmed trigger definitions rather than vague click or applicable wording.
The plan records which customer-space capabilities, values, pages, and implementation sources still require confirmation.
The browser attempt remains required and recorded even though screenshot delivery is explicitly excluded.

prohibited_outcomes.description: A gated page, capability, value, URL, or interaction is labelled observed or client-confirmed.
Public account labels or static discovery are treated as evidence that a specific gated event exists.
The entire customer-service journey is omitted solely because authentication was blocked.
